scenario_results.reason: Returned a mandate-specific control matrix tied to IMA, IPS, prospectus, restricted-list, and rule-coding evidence.
Missing holdings, transaction, and preclearance evidence correctly produced a blocked code-of-ethics evidence-gap summary.
Conflicting SEC, tenant, and FINRA marketing expectations were preserved and escalated instead of being flattened into a confident answer.
Requests for legal interpretation, regulator response, and formal signoff were rejected and routed to the proper owners.
Desk-practice conflict was captured as an exception and remediation issue rather than normalized.
The lane stayed blocked when mandate and rule-coding sources needed to interpret a breach were missing.
Market-abuse and restricted-list adjudication was routed to adjacent specialists with evidence references preserved.
Annual-review and supervisory evidence was packaged with cadence, owners, open gaps, and no unsupported certification claim.
Investment-specific terminology was normalized into mandate, marketing, code-of-ethics, and supervisory artifacts tied to real systems.
The pack refused to treat the industry label as cosmetic and demanded investment-specific evidence instead of generic policy artifacts.
Missing entity, jurisdiction, and system-hook facts correctly triggered low-confidence escalation.
Tenant-specific stricter controls were recorded without mutating the public baseline or improvising signoff.

notes: Scenario set covers mandate monitoring, books-and-records, code-of-ethics, marketing review, supervisory testing, missing evidence, policy conflict, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation handling.
All required shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios.
Runtime remains `specification-based` and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
